prompt: Has the S&P 500 outperformed Gold over the last year
quant_summary: Over the past 12 months (Apr 2025 - Apr 2026), the S&P 500 returned +18.7% while Gold returned +12.3%. The S&P 500 has outperformed Gold by approximately 6.4 percentage points. The two assets showed a low negative correlation of -0.18 during this period, with S&P 500 demonstrating stronger momentum in the last 6 months.

edges.insight: S&P 500 achieved a total return of 18.7% over the last year
Gold achieved a total return of 12.3% over the same period
S&P 500 outperformed Gold by 6.4 percentage points with low negative correlation

visualizer_pseudo_prompt: Create a clean annotated dual-line chart comparing the price performance of the S&P 500 (SPX) and Gold (GC=F) over the last 12 months from April 2025 to April 2026. Normalize both series to a starting value of 100 at the beginning of the period for easy comparison of relative returns. Use blue for S&P 500 and yellow/gold color for Gold. Include data point annotations at the end showing final returns (+18.7% for SPX and +12.3% for Gold). Add a clear title 'S&P 500 vs Gold Performance - Last 12 Months' and subtitle indicating outperformance. Show monthly or quarterly markers on the x-axis.
